regions.text: There are about five million red cells in one cubic millimeter of our blood. One millimeter is a thousandth part of a meter and a tenth of a centimeter.
One cubic centimeter—how much space does that take up? Here is a picture of a cube which is one centimeter long, one centimeter wide and one centimeter high.
A centimeter is a little less than half an inch. millimeter (mm): .001 of a meter. centimeter (cm): .01 of a meter. cube: solid with six equal square sides.
square: four equal sides and four right angles.
